File your dissertation

An overview of the UC Davis dissertation-filing process and key deadlines.

Professional Development

  • Attending a conference
  • Applying for funding
  • Request recommendation letters
  • File your dissertation

Once you’ve passed the qualifying exam, it’s time to think about submitting your dissertation and graduating — congratulations. UC Davis imposes specific requirements on this process; this page outlines what you need to know. (This page is a work in progress.)

Keep your committee updated

Your three-person dissertation committee should be kept abreast of your progress toward your final degree.

Write your dissertation

Compile your work into a comprehensive document. Note that UC Davis has strict formatting guidelines — see Preparing & filing your thesis or dissertation.

File your dissertation

Identify with Christina when you expect to graduate (by quarter). Work backward from that date:

  • Graduation − 3 quarters: begin planning milestones with your committee.
  • Graduation − 6 weeks: finalize the document against the formatting guidelines.
  • Graduation − 4 weeks: provide your dissertation to your committee.
  • Graduation: graduate!

Don’t forget to schedule your exit seminar — a dissertation cannot be signed without it.
